name: Companion Follow
description: An allied AI behavior where a companion maintains useful proximity to the player while avoiding obstruction, hazards, and unwanted combat mistakes.
design_notes: Companion follow is judged by how rarely it gets in the player's way. It needs follow_target_id, desired distance bands, teleport or catch-up rules, path_replan_interval_ms, combat override rules, and debug traces for why the companion chose a position.
design_purpose: Keep story, combat, or utility companions near enough to matter without blocking the player.
Support escort, co-op-like, or party fantasies with believable ally movement.
Let companions transition between following, helping, reviving, fighting, and waiting states.
player_fantasy: The player feels accompanied by a useful ally who keeps up and helps without becoming a navigation problem.

parameters.name: follow_target_id
parameters.description: Actor the companion tries to stay near or align with.

parameters.name: preferred_follow_distance
parameters.description: Distance band where the companion is considered comfortably following.

parameters.name: catchup_teleport_distance
parameters.description: Separation beyond which the companion may warp to a safe fallback point.

parameters.name: path_replan_interval_ms
parameters.description: Minimum interval between follow path recalculations.

parameters.name: obstruction_timeout_ms
parameters.description: Maximum blocked duration before using regroup, wait, or teleport fallback behavior.

edge_cases: The player jumps gaps, climbs ladders, enters vehicles, or uses traversal the companion cannot perform.
The companion blocks a doorway, loot pickup, camera view, or precision platforming path.
Combat starts while the companion is catching up or teleporting.
Save/load restores the companion in an invalid navmesh area or inside the player collider.
common_bugs: Companion replans every frame toward the player's exact position and jitters behind them.
Teleport fallback ignores line of sight or safe placement and appears in front of enemies or hazards.
Companion collision pushes the player off ledges or out of interact prompts.
Animation state says idle while navigation velocity is high, creating sliding allies.
balancing_notes: Companions should be useful enough to justify attention but not solve stealth, combat, or traversal for the player by default.
Catch-up rules should be generous in traversal-heavy games and stricter in games where escort positioning is the challenge.
Combat companions need separate threat, damage, and revive budgets from enemy AI so encounters remain readable.
accessibility_notes: Provide commands such as wait, follow, or regroup if companion positioning can interfere with motor or camera control.
Avoid companion chatter or alerts that are audio-only; important ally state should be visible.

implementation_notes.unity: Use NavMeshAgent with follow bands and sampled offsets rather than steering to the player's exact transform; log follow_reason, path status, and teleport fallback selection.
implementation_notes.godot: Use NavigationAgent2D/3D and a companion state machine with follow, wait, combat, and catchup states; collision layers should prevent allies from shoving the player.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AIController with Behavior Tree/StateTree Blackboard keys for FollowTarget, DesiredSlot, and CatchupState; smart links or teleport fallbacks should validate safe destinations.
implementation_notes.web: For grid or lightweight games, reserve adjacent follow cells and evaluate deterministic offsets by party order; persist companion state and fallback position for save/load.

example_games.title: The Last of Us
example_games.note: Uses companion characters that follow and assist during exploration and combat.

example_games.title: God of War
example_games.note: Uses an allied companion with follow and combat support behavior.
